pick.rationale: This 4/5 matchup features two top-20 teams with contrasting strengths and similar roster challenges—Texas Tech lost Big 12 Player of the Year JT Toppin to a season-ending ACL tear in February, while Alabama is without second-leading scorer Aden Holloway following his suspension for a felony drug arrest. The decisive factor is Texas Tech's elite defense ranking 27th nationally in adjusted defensive efficiency compared to Alabama's porous 67th-ranked defense, a 40-spot gap that should prove critical in tournament play where stops matter most. While Alabama boasts the nation's 3rd-ranked offense, Texas Tech's 9th-ranked attack is nearly as potent, and the Red Raiders have had over a month to adjust to life without Toppin behind All-American point guard Christian Anderson. Alabama's 80-79 SEC tournament loss to Ole Miss and defensive vulnerabilities give Texas Tech a narrow but meaningful edge in what projects as a closely contested game.

result.text: University of Alabama releases statement on Aden Holloway arrest Skip to main content Home TuscPreps College Columns TideSports.com SportsbookWire MMA Junkie Golf Week University of Alabama breaks silence on Aden Holloway arrest Emilee Smarr Tuscaloosa News Alabama basketball guard Aden Holloway has been suspended ahead of the Crimson Tide's sixth consecutive NCAA Tournament run. UA released a statement on Monday, March 16 after news surfaced Holloway, 21, was arrested for first-degree possession of marijuana and failure to affix a tax stamp. According to a spokesperson from the Tuscaloosa Police Department, Holloway's bond was set for $5,000 and he bonded out soon after his arrest on Monday morning. "The University is aware of the allegations and is working to gather more information. The student has been removed from campus pending further investigation by the UA Office of Student Conduct," the statement read. LOOKING AHEAD: Alabama basketball faces Hofstra to open 2026 NCAA tournament March Madness WHAT WE KNOW: Alabama basketball guard Aden Holloway arrested Holloway is a junior at the University of Alabama . He transferred from Auburn ahead of the 2024-25 season. Before his suspension, Holloway was averaging 16.8 points per game and was the Crimson Tide's best 3-point shooter, making nearly 44% of shots from beyond the arc. This included going a combined 9-of-29 (31%) from three-point range, as well as shooting only 41% from the field. Alabama was also out-rebounded a combined 42-36, struggled defensively, and never led against Ole Miss at any point in the contest, despite entering Friday night as a sizable favorite . Following the loss, Alabama head coach Nate Oats commented on the Crimson Tide's showing against the Rebels, sharing his thoughts on the disappointing showing overall during the opening statement of his postgame press conference . "I've got to give Ole Miss a lot of credit," Oats said. "I mean, they’re on a run. Coach Beard's coached in the national championship game before. He's a really good coach, knows what he’s doing. They had a tough regular season. He’s got them together, he's got them playing well. We tried to tell our guys this is a lot different team than what we saw in Oxford. They had us down in Oxford too, and then we figured some stuff out, went on a run and beat them, but this team is playing harder, more connected, more together. Looked to me like we didn’t think they were that much better than the first time we played them, despite the fact they just won two games." "A little disappointed with myself, just being able to convince our guys how good this team was going to be. They came out, we didn’t have an edge to us. They came out ready to play. They kind of killed us on the effort stuff. In the first half, everything was real comfortable for them. We didn’t play hard enough. We ended up getting out-rebounded in the game. We were getting out-rebounded by a large margin to start, and they got going. When you let a team that’s on a run, that's got some confidence behind them, get going, and you don’t play hard enough on defense and the effort stuff, you’re asking for a game like where you could end up losing." "I thought we played pretty hard for the last 10 minutes.
